chain.thought: Explanation: Consider unique singles and whether each reached the top 10 (position ≤10) on any national singles chart listed (Billboard Hot 100, Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs, Hot Dance Club Play). Only “i like it” (No. 7 Hot 100; No. 3 Dance Club Play) and “romeo” (No. 6 Hot 100) reached the top 10. The other 7 unique singles did not (positions >10 or “--”). Thus, the counts are 2 reached and 7 did not, which simultaneously maximizes the “reached top 10” tally (crediting any top-10 appearance) and minimizes the “did not reach” tally for the same set.

judge_thought: Explanation: The national singles chart here is the Billboard Hot 100. Counting songs that reached the Top 10 (positions 1–10): "i like it" (7) and "romeo" (6), totaling 2. The remaining Billboard Hot 100 entries—"summergirls" (50), "24/7" (42), "sunshine" (23), "never 2 much of u" (61), "gentle" (31), "ooh child" (27), and "endlessly" (--)—did not reach the Top 10, totaling 7. Therefore, the pair 2|7 is correct.
